title: fumaria rupestris subsp. bifrons
intro: Fumaria rupestris subsp. bifrons, also known as the limestone fumitory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the fumitory family, characterized by its delicate yellow flowers and ability to thrive in rocky, well-drained areas. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits associated with Fumaria rupestris subsp. bifrons include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to act as an expectorant and a diuretic. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
